item.title: AWS Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS)
item.description: Workshop - https://www.eksworkshop.com/docs/introduction/  Networking https://aws.github.io/aws-eks-best-practices/networking/  https://github.com/aws/amazon-vpc-cni-k8s  Advanced Networking https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/containers/eks-vpc-routable-ip-address-conservation/  can use both Fargate and EC2 clusters to launch nodes. STS endpoint for the deployment region must be enabled. worker nodes must have a private DNS entry or a node not found error will occur. node IAM role must be present in the aws-auth-cm.yaml file when using self-managed Amazon Linux nodes. Ref: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/eks/latest/userguide/troubleshooting.html  , https://docs.aws.amazon.com/eks/latest/userguide/eks-connector.html
